steps.title: Find the median of data set A
steps.content:
Data set A contains 21 values, so the median is the 11th value when ordered from smallest to largest.

Use cumulative counts from the frequency table:
  • 0 eagles: 1 day, so positions 1 through 1
  • 1 eagle: 3 days, so positions 2 through 4
  • 2 eagles: 4 days, so positions 5 through 8
  • 3 eagles: 5 days, so positions 9 through 13
The 11th value is in the 3-eagle group, so the median of data set A is 3.

steps.title: Find the median of data set B and compare
steps.content: Data set B has 20 values after 19 is removed, so the median is the average of the 10th and 11th values. Both are still in the 3-eagle group, so median B is $\frac{3+3}{2}=3$. Both medians equal 3, matching choice B.
